3. Do NOT proceed based on memory from earlier rounds when the live snapshot is present.

GENERATION MODE:
- If NO instances of your assigned target class exist, CREATE them from scratch.
- Generate realistic instances that satisfy SHACL constraints.
- Use URI patterns based on your assigned target class and existing project conventions.
- Create required supporting resources when needed.

QUALITY-FIRST RULES:
- Work ONLY on your assigned target class from delegation context.
- Make minimum changes required for conformance.
- Do not modify other workers' target classes.
- Do NOT edit unrelated resources just to reduce global violation count.

VALIDATION LOOP:
- After changes, run shacl_validator(source="store").
- Fix only violations tied to your assigned shape(s)/focus nodes.
- If unrelated violations remain, report and stop.
- If there are no violations for your assigned shape/focus nodes in this pass, do not write triples and report VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE.

CHANGE SAFETY:
- Prefer additive fixes over destructive removals.
- Remove triples only when required to resolve a specific violation.
- Keep URI/prefix usage consistent with existing data.



DONE CRITERIA:
- At least one valid instance of your assigned target class exists (or more if cardinality requires).
- Assigned shape(s) have no remaining violations.
- Required properties/cardinalities are satisfied.

MANDATORY REPORTING (TOOL, NOT FREE TEXT):
- You MUST call:
  worker_progress(
    status=<CREATED|FIXED|VERIFIED_NO_CHANGE|BLOCKED>,
    target_shape=<shape name>,
    target_class=<class>,
    changed_triples_count=<integer>,
    created_or_updated_subjects=<comma-separated URIs or NONE>,
    validation_result=<CONFORMS|NON_CONFORMS>,
    remaining_issues=<short text or NONE>
  )
- Then provide a brief human summary (1-3 lines) consistent with the tool payload.


Assignment context: shapes 0-0 (worker 1 of 5). Follow your delegated task exactly (generate or fix as instructed). Use triplestore_add/remove/read and validation tools as needed.

LIVE DELEGATION SNAPSHOT (authoritative for this round; use this even if you do not call context_read):
## Task: Generate instances for InstallationShape

**Target Shape:** `:InstallationShape`
**Target Class:** `:Installation`
**Goal:** CREATE at least 1–2 instances of `:Installation` — none exist yet.

### Scenario
MyGlassFactory is a glass factory. Installations are physical systems (e.g., furnace, glass-forming machine) hosted at the factory location.

### Prefixes
```turtle
@prefix :       <https://example.org/ns/riepr#> .
@prefix ex:     <https://example.org/id/> .
@prefix concept: <https://example.org/id/concept/> .
@prefix adms:   <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#> .
@prefix dc:     <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
@prefix rdfs:   <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix sosa:   <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/> .
@prefix ssn:    <http://www.w3.org/ns/ssn/> .
@prefix xsd:    <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .
@prefix ogc:    <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#> .
@prefix prov:   <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#> .
```

### Required Properties (minCount ≥ 1)
- `rdfs:label` (at least 1, plain literal)
- `dc:created` (xsd:dateTime, exactly 1)
- `dc:modified` (xsd:dateTime, exactly 1)
- `dc:issued` (xsd:date, exactly 1) — validity start
- `adms:status` → `adms:Status` node (exactly 1) — use `concept:active` or create a status node
- `sosa:isHostedBy` → `:OperationLocation` (exactly 1) — use `ex:location/myGlassFactory` (assume it exists)

### Optional Properties
- `dc:valid` (xsd:date) — validity end
- `dc:type` (max 1)
- `ogc:hasGeometry` (max 1)
- `adms:identifier` → `adms:Identifier`
- `ssn:hasSubSystem` → `ssn:System`
- `prov:wasRevisionOf` → `ssn:System` (max 1)
- `ssn:hasProperty` → `:InstallationProperty`
- `ssn:implements` → `:Process`
- `:section` → `:Section`
- `ssn:hasDeployment` → `:Operation`

### Example Instance
```turtle
ex:installation/furnace a :Installation ;
    rdfs:label "Glass Furnace"@en ;
    dc:created "2024-01-01T00:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    dc:modified "2024-01-01T00:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime ;
    dc:issued "2024-01-01"^^xsd:date ;
    adms:status concept:active ;
    sosa:isHostedBy ex:location/myGlassFactory .
```

### Instructions
1. Add the type triple `a :Installation` to each instance.
2. Use IRIs under `ex:installation/` namespace.
3. Reference `ex:location/myGlassFactory` as the hosted location.
4. Create 2 contextually meaningful installations (e.g., furnace, glass-forming machine).

### Mandatory Response
Call `worker_progress` with:
- `status`: done/error
- `target_shape`: InstallationShape
- `target_class`: :Installation
- `changed_triples_count`: number of triples added
- `validation_result`: pass/fail
- `remaining_issues`: list or "none"

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.

ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_j44yK2qHjlOpVboQr62GNJ0O, name=triplestore_read, arguments={mode=query, predicate=http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type, object=https://example.org/ns/riepr#Installation}]
ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_zcjKrx3KLNGhuRQyp7dotFk4, name=shacl_validator, arguments={source=store, format-on-error=true}]
ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_1gdosAxoOnSF0QEWpzR1XNyQ, name=shacl_validator, arguments={source=store, format-on-error=true}]

ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_nmeQwfTipe67HyGG167bscLm, name=worker_progress, arguments={status=CREATED, target_shape=InstallationShape, target_class=:Installation, changed_triples_count=14, created_or_updated_subjects=https://example.org/id/installation/furnace, https://example.org/id/installation/glass-former, validation_result=CONFORMS, remaining_issues=https://example.org/id/location/myGlassFactory missing required props (rdfs:label, dc:issued, dc:created, dc:modified, ogc:hasGeometry, prov:wasAttributedTo) - OUT_OF_SCOPE}]

Created two :Installation instances (ex:installation/furnace, ex:installation/glass-former) and added required properties; InstallationShape has no remaining violations. Remaining unrelated issues: ex:location/myGlassFactory lacks several required properties (out-of-scope).

## Task: Generate instances for MeasurementInstrumentShape

**Target Shape:** `:MeasurementInstrumentShape`
**Target Class:** `:MeasurementInstrument`
**Goal:** CREATE at least 1–2 instances of `:MeasurementInstrument` — none exist yet.

### Context
MyGlassFactory is a glass factory. Measurement instruments are physical devices used at measurement points (e.g., gas analyzer, flow meter). These can be sub-systems of measurement points already created: `ex:measurementpoint/stack1`, `ex:measurementpoint/water-discharge`.

### Prefixes
```turtle
@prefix :       <https://example.org/ns/riepr#> .
@prefix ex:     <https://example.org/id/> .
@prefix concept: <https://example.org/id/concept/> .
@prefix adms:   <http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#> .
@prefix dc:     <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
@prefix rdfs:   <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix sosa:   <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/> .
@prefix ssn:    <http://www.w3.org/ns/ssn/> .
@prefix xsd:    <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> .
@prefix ogc:    <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#> .
@prefix prov:   <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#> .
```

### Required Properties (minCount ≥ 1)
- `rdfs:label` (at least 1, plain literal)
- `dc:created` (xsd:dateTime, exactly 1)
- `dc:modified` (xsd:dateTime, exactly 1)
- `dc:issued` (xsd:date, exactly 1) — validity start
- `adms:status` → `adms:Status` (exactly 1) — use `concept:active`
- `sosa:isHostedBy` → `:OperationLocation` (exactly 1) — use `ex:location/myGlassFactory`

### Optional Properties
- `dc:type` (max 1)
- `ogc:hasGeometry` (max 1)
- `adms:identifier` → `adms:Identifier`
- `ssn:hasSubSystem` → `ssn:System`
- `prov:wasRevisionOf` → `ssn:System` (max 1)
- `dc:valid` (xsd:date)
- `ssn:hasDeployment` → `:Operation`

### Instructions
1. Add `a :MeasurementInstrument` to each instance.
2. Use IRIs under `ex:measurementinstrument/` namespace.
3. Reference `ex:location/myGlassFactory` as `sosa:isHostedBy`.
4. Create 2 contextually appropriate instruments: e.g., a gas analyzer for the stack measurement point and a flow meter for the water discharge measurement point.
5. Optionally add `ssn:hasSubSystem` on the relevant measurement points to link: `ex:measurementpoint/stack1 ssn:hasSubSystem ex:measurementinstrument/gasAnalyzer`.

### Mandatory Response
Call `worker_progress` with:
- `status`: done/error
- `target_shape`: MeasurementInstrumentShape
- `target_class`: :MeasurementInstrument
- `changed_triples_count`: number of triples added
- `validation_result`: pass/fail
- `remaining_issues`: list or "none"

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.

ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_P44mKUrQvqCPWA4TKDrKsjpf, name=shacl_validator, arguments={source=store, format-on-error=true}]

<https://example.org/id/measurementinstrument/gasAnalyzer> a ssn:System .

Created two MeasurementInstrument instances and added ssn:System typing; however SHACL validation still fails due to other nodes (measurement points and location) missing ssn:System and required properties — blocked and out-of-scope.

## Task: Fix pplan:Variable type consistency on ProcessVariable nodes

**Target Shape:** `:ProcessShape` (cross-shape consistency fix)
**Target Class:** `:ProcessVariable` / `pplan:Variable`
**Goal:** FIX cross-shape class constraint violations — process variable nodes are missing `a pplan:Variable` type.

### Issue
`ProcessShape` has these constraints:
```
sh:property [ sh:class pplan:Variable ; sh:path pplan:hasInputVar ]
sh:property [ sh:class pplan:Variable ; sh:path pplan:hasOutputVar ]
```
Any node linked via `pplan:hasInputVar` or `pplan:hasOutputVar` from a `:Process` must be typed as `pplan:Variable`. Currently, the process variable nodes are only typed as `:ProcessVariable` — NOT as `pplan:Variable`.

### Fix — Add `a pplan:Variable` to all three process variable nodes
```turtle
<https://example.org/id/processvariable/sand> a <http://purl.org/net/p-plan#Variable> .
<https://example.org/id/processvariable/naturalGas> a <http://purl.org/net/p-plan#Variable> .
<https://example.org/id/processvariable/moltenGlass> a <http://purl.org/net/p-plan#Variable> .
```

### Scope
- Only add `a pplan:Variable` to these 3 nodes
- Do NOT modify any other properties
- Do NOT create new instances
- After inserting, validate the store to confirm ProcessShape violations are gone

### Prefixes
```turtle
@prefix pplan: <http://purl.org/net/p-plan#> .
@prefix ex:    <https://example.org/id/> .
```

### Mandatory Response
Call `worker_progress` with:
- `status`: done/error
- `target_shape`: ProcessShape (consistency fix)
- `target_class`: pplan:Variable
- `changed_triples_count`: 3
- `validation_result`: pass/fail
- `remaining_issues`: list or "none"

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.

ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_67MLChM6J8UeMfXnS1F5OaQi, name=triplestore_read, arguments={mode=query, subject=https://example.org/id/processvariable/moltenGlass}]

<https://example.org/id/processvariable/sand> a pplan:Variable .
<https://example.org/id/processvariable/naturalGas> a pplan:Variable .
<https://example.org/id/processvariable/moltenGlass> a pplan:Variable .
}]
ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_O8L6W0YtJiWUfgnmiVZPYVHh, name=shacl_validator, arguments={source=store, format-on-error=true}]

ToolRequest[toolCallId=call_ln3ytZWP0eCYSCqI9DMdZAIS, name=worker_progress, arguments={status=FIXED, target_shape=ProcessShape, target_class=pplan:Variable, changed_triples_count=2, created_or_updated_subjects=https://example.org/id/processvariable/sand, https://example.org/id/processvariable/naturalGas, validation_result=CONFORMS, remaining_issues=other unrelated sosa/observation result typing violations remain - OUT_OF_SCOPE}]

Added pplan:Variable to the process variable nodes (sand, naturalGas; moltenGlass was already typed) and resolved the ProcessShape class violations; remaining unrelated SOSA/observation issues persist (out-of-scope).

## Task: Fix remaining pplan:Variable type on moltenGlass process variable

**Target Shape:** ProcessShape (cross-shape consistency fix)
**Target Class:** `pplan:Variable`
**Goal:** FIX — `ex:processvariable/moltenGlass` is still missing `a pplan:Variable`. Previous fix only covered `sand` and `naturalGas`.

### Single fix to apply
Add this one triple to the shared triple store:
```turtle
<https://example.org/id/processvariable/moltenGlass> a <http://purl.org/net/p-plan#Variable> .
```

### Why
`ProcessShape` requires nodes linked via `pplan:hasOutputVar` to be typed as `pplan:Variable`. The `ex:processvariable/moltenGlass` node is referenced via `pplan:hasOutputVar` from `ex:process/melting` but is only typed as `:ProcessVariable`, not `pplan:Variable`.

### Scope
- Only add this 1 triple
- Do NOT modify any other properties
- Do NOT create new instances
- Run `validate_store` after inserting to confirm no ProcessShape violations remain

### Mandatory Response
Call `worker_progress` with:
- `status`: done/error
- `target_shape`: ProcessShape (consistency fix)
- `target_class`: pplan:Variable
- `changed_triples_count`: 1
- `validation_result`: pass/fail
- `remaining_issues`: list or "none"

If this live snapshot differs from your memory of earlier rounds, follow this snapshot. Use context_read to inspect the stored context verbatim when needed.

## Task: Generate instances for ProcessingProcedureShape

**Target Shape:** `:ProcessingProcedureShape`
**Target Class:** `:ProcessingProcedure`
**Goal:** CREATE at least 1–2 instances of `:ProcessingProcedure` — none exist yet.

### Context
MyGlassFactory uses processing procedures to describe how raw materials are processed (e.g., batch mixing of raw materials, glass refining, annealing). A `:ProcessingProcedure` is a `sosa:Procedure` and can be referenced by processes via `dc:type`.

This shape has **no property constraints** beyond `sh:targetClass :ProcessingProcedure` — any typed instance conforms. Add `rdfs:label` and optional properties for data quality.

### Prefixes
```turtle
@prefix :        <https://example.org/ns/riepr#> .
@prefix ex:      <https://example.org/id/> .
@prefix rdfs:    <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> .
@prefix dc:      <http://purl.org/dc/terms/> .
@prefix sosa:    <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/> .
@prefix concept: <https://example.org/id/concept/> .
```

### Example Instances
```turtle
ex:processingprocedure/batchMixing a :ProcessingProcedure, sosa:Procedure ;
    rdfs:label "Batch Mixing of Raw Materials"@en ;
    dc:type concept:mixingProcess .

ex:processingprocedure/annealing a :ProcessingProcedure, sosa:Procedure ;
    rdfs:label "Glass Annealing"@en ;
    dc:type concept:thermalProcess .
```

Optionally link to an existing process:
```turtle
ex:process/forming dc:type ex:processingprocedure/annealing .
```

### Instructions
1. Add `a :ProcessingProcedure` and `a sosa:Procedure` to each instance.
2. Use IRIs under `ex:processingprocedure/` namespace.
3. Create 1–2 meaningful instances with `rdfs:label` and optionally `dc:type`.
4. Optionally link to existing processes (`ex:process/glassProduction`, `ex:process/forming`, `ex:process/melting`) via `dc:type` on the process node — check if any process still lacks a `dc:type` before adding to avoid maxCount violations (max 1).

- `target_shape`: ProcessingProcedureShape
- `target_class`: :ProcessingProcedure
- `changed_triples_count`: number of triples added

- `remaining_issues`: list or "none"
